title: Full Bathroom Remodel

budget.description: Basic updates - refinish tub, new vanity, repaint, new hardware, vinyl flooring

midRange.description: New tile shower, new vanity with quartz top, porcelain tile floor, new toilet, updated lighting

premium.description: Custom walk-in shower with glass door, freestanding tub, heated floor, custom vanity, premium tile throughout

costFactors.title: Shower/Tub Choice
costFactors.description: This is the single biggest cost swing in a bathroom remodel. A prefab fiberglass tub-shower combo runs $200-$800 installed. A custom tile walk-in shower with frameless glass door pushes $5,000-$15,000. If you can keep your existing tub and just refinish it for $300-$600, you save thousands compared to a full replacement.

costFactors.title: Tile Selection
costFactors.description: Basic ceramic tile starts at $0.50-$2 per square foot for materials. Porcelain runs $3-$10. Natural stone like marble or travertine jumps to $15-$35 per square foot. For a typical 75 sq ft bathroom with floor and shower walls, tile costs alone can range from $500 to $5,000+ depending on your choice.

costFactors.title: Vanity Quality
costFactors.description: A stock vanity from a big-box store runs $300-$800 including the top. Semi-custom options with quartz or stone tops land at $800-$2,000. Fully custom vanities with premium countertops and undermount sinks push $2,000-$3,800. Double vanities in a master bath cost roughly 50-75% more than a single.

costFactors.title: Plumbing Changes
costFactors.description: Keeping fixtures in their current locations saves thousands. The moment you relocate a toilet, sink, or shower drain, you're looking at $1,500-$4,000 per fixture for new supply lines and drain routing. A full plumbing relayout in a master bath can hit $10,000-$15,000 in plumbing alone.

costFactors.title: Bathroom Size
costFactors.description: A small half bath (35 sq ft) remodels for $2,500-$8,000. A standard full bath (75 sq ft) runs $6,500-$18,000. A large master bathroom (150-200 sq ft) with separate shower, tub, and double vanity can easily reach $25,000-$55,000. Cost per square foot generally ranges from $75 to $275.

materialOptions.pros: Most affordable tile option, wide variety of colors and styles
materialOptions.cons: Less durable than porcelain, can chip more easily

materialOptions.pros: Very durable, low water absorption, works for floors and walls
materialOptions.cons: Harder to cut and install than ceramic, higher cost
materialOptions.bestFor: Most popular mid-range choice for wet areas

materialOptions.pros: Luxury look, unique patterns, high resale value
materialOptions.cons: Requires sealing, can stain, higher maintenance
Looks budget, limited design options, yellows over time

materialOptions.pros: Fully customizable design, premium look, durable
materialOptions.cons: Expensive, longer install time, requires proper waterproofing

westCoast.label: West Coast
westCoast.note: Highest labor costs nationally, strict permitting

midwest.label: Midwest
midwest.note: Lowest costs nationally, good contractor availability

diyVsPro.diyRisks: Waterproofing mistakes are the biggest risk. A poorly sealed shower will leak into walls and subfloor, causing mold and structural damage that costs $3,000-$10,000+ to remediate. Plumbing errors can flood your home, and improper electrical work in a wet environment is a serious safety hazard.

savingsTips: Keep your existing layout. Moving plumbing fixtures is where costs spike - keeping the toilet, sink, and shower in place saves $2,000-$8,000.
Refinish your tub instead of replacing it. Professional refinishing costs $300-$600 vs. $1,500-$3,000+ for a new tub with installation.
Choose porcelain tile over natural stone. Porcelain delivers a similar high-end look at $12-$30/sq ft vs. $20-$50 for natural stone, and it requires no sealing.
Use a shower kit instead of custom tile. A quality acrylic shower kit runs $800-$2,000 installed vs. $2,500-$10,000 for a custom tile shower.
Shop floor model and clearance vanities. Big-box stores and kitchen/bath showrooms discount display models 30-50% off retail.
Do your own demolition. Ripping out old tile, vanity, and fixtures saves $300-$1,000 in labor. Just be careful around plumbing connections.
Skip heated floors in a small bathroom. For bathrooms under 50 sq ft, a quality bath mat achieves the same comfort without the $800-$2,500 install cost.
Choose a standard toilet over a wall-mounted model. Standard toilets run $150-$400 installed vs. $1,000-$2,500 for wall-mounted units that require in-wall carriers.

contractorQuestions.question: What waterproofing method do you use in the shower area?
contractorQuestions.why: Proper waterproofing (Kerdi membrane, RedGard, or similar) is critical. If a contractor can't explain their waterproofing system in detail, that's a red flag.

contractorQuestions.question: How do you handle mold or water damage discovered during demo?
contractorQuestions.why: Mold behind old tile and rotted subfloors are common surprises. You need to know their process and pricing for remediation before work begins.

contractorQuestions.question: Will you inspect the subfloor before tiling?
contractorQuestions.why: Tile installed on a weak or uneven subfloor will crack. A good contractor checks for deflection and levelness and will add cement board or replace damaged sections.

contractorQuestions.question: What ventilation improvements do you recommend?
contractorQuestions.why: Proper bathroom ventilation prevents future mold issues. A contractor who doesn't mention upgrading your exhaust fan may be cutting corners.

contractorQuestions.question: What's your payment schedule?
contractorQuestions.why: Red flag if they want more than 10-15% upfront. Standard is a deposit, then progress payments tied to milestones like rough-in completion and tile completion.

contractorQuestions.question: Are you licensed, insured, and bonded?
contractorQuestions.why: Verify their license number with your state licensing board. Insurance protects you if a worker is injured in your home.

contractorQuestions.question: What warranty do you offer on labor and waterproofing?
contractorQuestions.why: Most reputable contractors offer 1-2 year warranties on workmanship. Waterproofing failures can be catastrophic, so ask specifically about that coverage.

secondaryKeywords: how much does a bathroom remodel cost
seo.metaDescription: How much does a bathroom remodel cost in 2026? National average is $12,000. Get detailed cost breakdowns by tier, material comparisons, regional pricing, and an interactive calculator.
